creator: terry-freebsd@glaver.org
text:
Created attachment 256679
dmesg extract when booting with mfi(4)

In switching from the mfi(4) to mrsas(4), the backplane on a Dell PowerEdge R730 with a PERC H730P controller disappears.

Attached are two kernel boot log extracts (grepping for ^ses) from the same hardware and the same kernel (14.2-STABLE), once with the mfi(4) driver and once with the mrsas(4) driver, switched with the usual hw.mfi.mrsas_enable=1 tunable in /boot/loader.conf.

Losing the ses device for the main drive backplane means that I cannot monitor drive stats, so this is more than an inconvenience.
